skill.name: app-store-optimization
skill.description: App Store Optimization toolkit for keyword research, metadata optimization, competitor analysis, ASO scoring, A/B test planning, localization, review analysis, and launch preparation.
skill.summary: Optimize mobile app store listings for organic discovery and conversion.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 431 potential issues, but review found no confirmed malicious behavior. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by ASO terminology and dictionary access patterns, the external command alerts are Markdown code fences and inline code, and the filesystem/network alerts are documentation paths or ordinary review-analysis text. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, subprocess execution, file manipulation, or outbound network calls.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Alerts
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged files use ASO scoring terms, dictionaries, thresholds, and marketing calculations. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, encryption, password hashing, token handling, or other cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found only ASO domain logic and standard data structures at representative flagged locations. A targeted search found no cryptographic APIs or secret-handling code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown fences and inline code examples for prompts, JSON samples, and function names. They are documentation text and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown documentation blocks or inline code references. No shell, Ruby, subprocess, or dynamic command invocation was found in implementation files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden Filesystem Path Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hidden path references document standard Claude skill installation and verification paths. The Python modules do not read from or write to these paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are install and verification examples in documentation. A code search found no file access APIs in the Python modules.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Request Terminology
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The network alerts are caused by the English word 'requests' in review-analysis feature names and comments. No evidence found of HTTP libraries, sockets, fetch calls, webhooks, or outbound data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The implementation imports only typing, collections, and re for review text processing. The flagged occurrences refer to feature requests, not network requests.

content.value_statement: Mobile teams need clear ASO guidance without building spreadsheets from scratch. This skill turns app data into keyword, metadata, competitor, testing, localization, and launch recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes keyword volume, competition, relevance, density, and long-tail opportunities.
Creates Apple and Google metadata options with platform character limit checks.
Compares competitor metadata, keywords, ratings, and positioning signals.
Calculates ASO health scores across metadata, ratings, keywords, and conversion metrics.
Plans A/B tests with sample size, duration, confidence, and success metrics.
Generates localization plans, review insights, response templates, and launch checklists.
limitations: Search volume and competitor metrics must be supplied or estimated by the user.
The modules do not connect to App Store Connect, Google Play Console, or live store APIs.
Recommendations must be checked against current Apple and Google policies before release.
The skill focuses on organic ASO and does not manage paid acquisition campaigns.

use_cases.title: Prepare a new app launch
use_cases.description: Research launch keywords, build store metadata, and generate a pre-submission checklist for both major app stores.

use_cases.title: Improve an existing listing
use_cases.description: Score current ASO performance, find weak areas, and prioritize metadata or visual tests.

use_cases.title: Turn reviews into roadmap input
use_cases.description: Analyze public review text for sentiment, repeated issues, requested features, and response templates.

prompt_templates.title: Find launch keywords
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner keyword research for a new app
prompt_templates.prompt: Research ASO keywords for my [category] app. Audience: [audience]. Features: [features]. Competitors: [competitors]. Return primary, secondary, and long-tail keyword ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize store metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize my app metadata for [Apple App Store, Google Play, or both]. App name: [name]. Current metadata: [paste]. Target keywords: [keywords]. Validate character limits.

prompt_templates.title: Compare competitors
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these competitors for ASO positioning: [competitor list]. Compare titles, descriptions, keywords, ratings, and gaps my app can target.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an ASO experiment
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an A/B test for my [icon, screenshot, title, or description]. Baseline conversion is [rate]. Daily visitors are [number]. Include sample size, duration, metrics, and decision rules.

output_examples.input: Research keywords for a fitness app with home workouts and meal planning.
output: Primary keywords focus on home workout, fitness planner, and meal tracker.
Long-tail opportunities include workout plan at home and fitness meal schedule.
Recommendation: use the strongest functional keyword in the title and broader terms in the description.

output_examples.input: Calculate an ASO score for a productivity app with ratings, keyword rankings, and conversion data.
output: Overall ASO score is presented with separate metadata, ratings, keyword, and conversion components.
The highest priority improvement is expanding keywords ranked in the top 50.
Secondary actions include improving description length and asking satisfied users for ratings.

output_examples.input: Plan an icon A/B test with a 4.2 percent baseline conversion rate.
output: The test plan defines a control icon, variation icon, conversion metric, and minimum detectable effect.
Sample size and duration are estimated for low, medium, and high traffic levels.
The decision rule requires statistical confidence and no major store event during the test window.

best_practices: Provide real app metrics, current metadata, competitors, and target markets before asking for recommendations.
Optimize Apple and Google listings separately because their metadata rules and ranking signals differ.
Validate every recommendation against current store policies before submitting production metadata.
anti_patterns: Targeting high-volume keywords that do not accurately describe the app.
Changing titles, screenshots, and icons at the same time without a test plan.
Using estimated ASO scores as proof of performance without tracking real store results.

faq.question: Does this skill fetch live app store data?
faq.answer: No. It analyzes data you provide and uses built-in ASO heuristics. Supply current metrics for better recommendations.

faq.question: Which stores does it support?
faq.answer: It supports Apple App Store and Google Play Store metadata, scoring, testing, localization, review, and launch workflows.

faq.question: Can it write Apple keyword fields?
faq.answer: Yes. It can build Apple keyword fields within the 100-character limit and remove duplicate terms.

faq.question: Can it analyze user reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide review text, ratings, and dates. It can summarize sentiment, issues, themes, and feature requests.

faq.question: Is it useful before launch?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create keyword research, metadata drafts, launch timelines, compliance checklists, and testing plans.

faq.question: Does it replace professional localization?
faq.answer: No. It can plan localization and adapt metadata, but native review is recommended before publishing translated listings.
